Continued non-convergence is itself data — AI-assisted alternative cosmologies are not networking on published surfaces, even when both actors are highly active in their respective modalities. no_action_gaps.gap: Aether-cosmology has 19 stars / 10 forks vs our 0 stars on the dome-model-review repo (delta this run: +2 / +1).
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Stars/forks on a critical-review repo are not the discoverability metric we should optimize for. The cosmology-skeptic audience reaches us via search, not GitHub stargazers. Optimizing for repo stars would distort tone toward debate-bait. Right metric is GSC click-through-rate + Fact Check Explorer surfaces + inbound links from authoritative sources, all tracked already. (Caveat per yesterday's contrarian read: this is the right principle, but we don't currently have a low-distortion path to stars/forks growth either; the deprioritization is partly a rationalization for inaction in an area we don't have a playbook for.)

no_action_gaps.gap: Aether-cosmology added Chinese-language display units today (du/fen/li in S757, S767), expanding their audience axis to Chinese-reading flat-earth communities. We have no multilingual content.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Translating a critical review into Chinese is a substantial content-side effort with low expected return given (a) we're not yet getting clicks even on English content, (b) Chinese-language flat-earth communities are not our pre-defined audience, and (c) the dome model itself is not Chinese-language. If the dome model later adds Chinese content, revisit. For now, no action.

contrarian_read.claim: Day 10 of registry-tally HOLD + Day 14 of machine-layer staleness are meaningfully significant as 'NEW RECORDS.'
contrarian_read.counter: They're new records in our LOG only. Our log goes back to early April 2026; we don't have density data on registry-iteration vs. quiet-period before that. The author was iterating actively in March 2026 but the visible activity reflected on our log starts ~2026-04-07. Calling these 'records' implies novelty against a longer baseline; honest framing is 'longest within our N≈30-day observation window.' This might just be the dome's normal rate-of-iteration when not in active-rebuild mode. The pattern is interesting but the 'record' framing is overstated.

contrarian_read.claim: TLS recurrence today triggers tinker proposal per yesterday's escalation criterion.
contrarian_read.counter: Yesterday's specific criterion was '3rd consecutive day = file proposal' which Apr 30 broke. The contrarian addendum was 'if failure returns within 7 days, 3-of-N pattern is worth filing.' But '3-of-N pattern' is not a clearly-defined statistical threshold — could just be intermittent sandbox/edge-cycling unrelated to the site. A counter-position: wait for 4-of-7 or 5-of-10 before filing. The tinker proposal will be cheap (one diagnostic JSON file) so the cost of filing-too-early is low; but I should be honest that this is my-call-not-clearly-warranted-by-prior-criterion. Logging this contrarian counter explicitly so operator can override if it's overzealous.

contrarian_read.claim: Aether-cosmology adding Chinese-language UI is a notable new content axis worth flagging.
contrarian_read.counter: It's notable as raw observation, but its strategic relevance to OUR work is near zero. We don't review aether-cosmology, our audience isn't Chinese-reading, and the Chinese-units feature doesn't bring aether-cosmology into convergence with the dome model. It's interesting solo-dev behavior but functionally irrelevant to the dome review. The discipline is to log new axes for future cross-actor convergence detection, but I should be honest that 'NEW CONTENT AXIS DETECTED' framing inflates its importance to our work.

contrarian_read.claim: site:funwithscience.net returning NO RESULTS today is an issue worth flagging.
contrarian_read.counter: It might just be Google's per-session SERP variance — the site: operator is known to return inconsistent results across sessions and time-of-day, especially for new domains with low authority. A single observation is not an issue; it's noise. The right discipline is 'check next 1-2 runs; if persistent across 3 days, file.' Flagging today as an 'observation' (not an action) gets this right, but I should resist the tendency to escalate single SERP-quirk observations into pseudo-issues.
